mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payroll employment indicates expanding labor demand and income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive 20-day forward returns for the S&P 500.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS declines month-over-month and forward returns are positive in over 60% of such instances, or if the rank IC over a 2-year window is below 0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate of at least 50 basis points over the past 3 months signals a shift to accommodative monetary policy, reducing discount rates and boosting equity valuations, leading to positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If after such a decline, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p>0.05) or the win rate is below 55%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is low (below 4.5%) and the consumer price index is rising (year-over-year change above 3%), it indicates an overheating economy that will prompt monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If, when both conditions hold, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the negative signal strength (average return conditional on trigger) is not significantly below unconditional average, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (unemployment rate below 4%), any further decline in the unemployment rate signals rising wage pressure and potential Fed tightening, which reduces equity valuations, leading to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: If, during periods when UNRATE < 4%, the correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p<0.05), or if in the opposite regime (UNRATE > 6%) the correlation is also negative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing activity, which weakens earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO decreases month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: If, after such a signal, the average 5-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p<0.05) or the signal occurs at least 50 times and the win rate for negative returns is below 55%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in retail and food services sales indicates robust consumer demand, which drives revenue growth across sectors and supports positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: If RSAFS decreases year-over-year and forward returns are still positive in more than 60% of such periods, or if the rank IC over 3 years is below 0.03, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts are increasing (3-month moving average rise) and the federal funds rate remains low (below 2.5%), it signals a supportive environment for residential investment and economic growth, which is positive for equity markets.
failure_condition: If, when both conditions hold, the 20-day forward return is negative more often than not, or if the average return is not significantly above the unconditional mean, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate (more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month period) signals a deteriorating labor market, reducing consumer spending and earnings, leading to negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If, after such a rise, the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p<0.05) or if the signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 55% of cases over out-of-sample periods, the hypothesis is rejected.
